headline: Chrome's global Gemini shortcut signals AI integration into everyday workflows
body: Google Chrome installed a global Ctrl+G keyboard shortcut that launches Gemini directly from any application, reflecting the broader industry push to embed AI assistants into standard user interfaces. For healthcare organizations, this pattern matters because it demonstrates how AI tools will increasingly surface within clinical workflows whether vendors plan for it or not. Healthcare IT teams must now consider how employees will access generative AI during patient care, what data exposure this creates in EHRs and messaging systems, and whether organization-wide AI policies can govern shortcuts that bypass traditional security controls. This also signals a shift in the vendor landscape where browser-based AI access may compete with purpose-built clinical decision support tools, forcing healthcare consulting practices to advise clients on integration strategies rather than replacement scenarios.
